name: Root cause analysis for intermittent null pointer
description: Evaluate the skill's ability to conduct a structured root cause analysis for an intermittent NullPointerException in a multi-threaded service, applying the Five Whys and evidence-based investigation.
prompt: Our user-service throws a NullPointerException about 5% of the time when handling GET /api/users/:id requests. It only happens under load (>100 concurrent requests). The stack trace points to UserCache.get() returning null. The cache is a ConcurrentHashMap populated on startup. Walk me through a systematic debugging process to find the root cause.

assertions.description: Should start by establishing reliable reproduction steps under load conditions
Should form a specific, falsifiable hypothesis about the cause (e.g., race condition, cache eviction)
Should apply the Five Whys or equivalent root cause analysis technique
Should investigate thread safety and concurrent access patterns as a likely root cause
Should recommend a regression test that verifies the fix under concurrent conditions

name: Systematic bisection for deployment regression
description: Evaluate the skill's ability to apply systematic bisection (git bisect or equivalent) to identify the exact commit that introduced a performance regression after a release.
prompt: After deploying version 2.4.0 (containing 47 commits), our API response time jumped from 150ms to 800ms. We need to find the responsible commit. Design a systematic bisection strategy. The commits span database changes, new middleware, dependency updates, and feature code. Show the bisection process, how to define good/bad criteria, and how to handle commits that can't be tested in isolation.

assertions.description: Should recommend git bisect or equivalent binary search strategy to narrow from 47 commits
Should define clear good/bad criteria based on measurable response time threshold
Should address how to handle untestable commits (git bisect skip or manual workaround)
Should suggest using logarithmic narrowing (O(log n) steps) for efficiency
Should recommend automating the bisection with a test script for consistent evaluation
